dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6940 IH: Hope Heals Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Biggs of South Carolina introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to assess how Executive departments address mental health crisis prevention and awareness, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Hope Heals Act of 2026.

section.header: Mental health crisis prevention and awareness

paragraph.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Health and Human Services (in this section referred to as the Secretary) shall assess—

subparagraph.text: the feasibility of improving coordination between Executive departments that provide direct services to the public, with respect to identifying—

clause.text: any individual experiencing a mental health crisis; or
any individual that expresses concern that they may be experiencing such a crisis or related distress;

subparagraph.text: any opportunity for an Executive department to improve how such department shares information, best practices, and referral mechanisms related to suicide prevention and mental health support with an individual seeking services during a mental health crisis; and
for the purpose of increasing awareness of the signs of a mental health crisis, whether an Executive department may implement a program to distribute—

clause.text: a validated screening tool, including the Patient Health Questionnaire-3 (PHQ–3) or a comparable tool; and

paragraph.text: In carrying out the assessment under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall coordinate with the head of any relevant Executive department, which may include—
In carrying out the assessment under paragraph (1), the Secretary may consult with—

subparagraph.text: an independent person with expertise in suicide prevention, public mental health, behavioral health systems, or coordination between Executive departments;
an academic institution with demonstrated expertise in mental or behavioral health research, suicide prevention, or public health;
any such other entity with relevant expertise.

Not later than 180 days after the date on which the Secretary completes the assessment under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall submit to each appropriate congressional committee a report containing the results of such assessment and any recommendations related to such results.
In this paragraph, the term appropriate congressional committee means—

clause.text: the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives;
the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate; and
any congressional committee with jurisdiction over the head of an Executive department that the Secretary coordinates with under paragraph (2).

subsection.text: Each Executive department shall, to the extent practicable, implement any recommendation contained within the report submitted under subsection (a)(4).

paragraph.text: The term Executive department means an Executive department listed in section 101 of title 5, United States Code.
The term mental health crisis means a situation in which an individual—

subparagraph.text: experiences acute psychological distress or behavioral disruption that—

clause.text: places such individual at risk of harming themself or another individual; or
significantly impairs the ability of such individual to function in daily life; and

subparagraph.text: requires support, intervention, or referral to services.
